sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Intercont (Cayman) Limited 2026 Strategic Plan Release - Anchoring the Core Shipping Business, Multi-Sector Synergy Opens New Growth Trajectories
feed.summary: Intercont (Cayman) Limited released its 2026 strategic plan, focusing on anchoring its core shipping business while expanding into high-potential sectors. The company plans to grow its green ro-ro vessel shipping business, acquire the Web3 enterprise zCloak, and enter the Artificial Intelligence (AI) field. This strategy aims to create a "Green Shipping + Web3 & AI Infrastructure" growth matrix, leveraging Turkey as a hub for AI infrastructure deployment to build a platform with long-term growth characteristics.

feed.title: FICO stock hits 52-week low at 1284.03 USD
feed.summary: Fair Isaac and Company Inc. (FICO) stock has reached a new 52-week low at $1284.03, reflecting a 28.43% decline over the past year, despite the company's strong gross profit margins and profitability. InvestingPro analysis suggests FICO is slightly undervalued with a "GOOD" financial health rating, even though it trades at a high P/E ratio.

feed.title: January 2026 Is Strongest Booking Month in Regent Seven Seas Cruises History
feed.summary: Regent Seven Seas Cruises experienced its strongest booking month in history this January, with a 20% increase in bookings compared to January 2025. This record performance was driven by high demand for global destinations, particularly Pacific and South America itineraries, and a trend towards longer voyages and multiple cruise bookings. The success was aided by the "Upgrade Your Horizon Wave Season" offer.

feed.title: Xcel Stock in 2026: What the Numbers Say After 15% Returns Last Year
feed.summary: Xcel Energy in 2026 is projected to see continued growth, driven by an ambitious expansion of data center capacity from 3 GW to 6 GW by 2027 and a significant $60 billion capital investment plan. Despite these growth catalysts and past positive returns, a TIKR valuation model suggests a "Sell" recommendation, as the estimated 8.2% annualized return on Xcel stock falls short of a 10% equity hurdle rate, indicating potential for capital preservation rather than substantial risk-adjusted appreciation. The company's future performance hinges on successful execution of its infrastructure projects, regulatory approvals, and disciplined cost management.

feed.title: Is Costco Stock a Buy Now or Still Too Expensive to Touch?
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation is trading at a premium valuation with a forward 12-month P/E of 46.21, significantly higher than industry and S&P 500 averages, making it an expensive stock despite strong fundamentals. While its membership model and consistent sales growth provide resilience, analysts suggest a "Hold" rating, advising existing investors to maintain positions and new investors to await a more attractive entry point. The company continues to show robust performance, outperforming some peers year-to-date, but its high valuation warrants caution.

feed.title: American Electric Power: Ideally Positioned to Benefit From Growing Demand
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is seen as ideally positioned to benefit from growing demand, with its business strategy focused on regulated investments across numerous utilities. This strategy is expected to support management's 7% to 9% earnings growth target for 2026-2030, which analysts view as achievable. Recent developments in the PJM market are also considered positive for regulated transmission and distribution utilities like AEP.

feed.title: International Paper - Director/PDMR Shareholding - Replacement
feed.summary: International Paper issued a replacement announcement regarding Director/PDMR shareholdings, specifically correcting the number of shares withheld for taxes for Timothy S. Nicholls. The initial announcement on February 4, 2026, incorrectly calculated tax withholdings for Nicholls, an expatriate, due to an administrative error regarding federal taxes. This updated notification restates the transaction details for Nicholls and includes vesting information for W. Thomas Hamic and Joseph R. Saab.

feed.title: Stifel raises Generac stock price target to $235 on data center growth
feed.summary: Stifel has increased its price target for Generac Holdings (NYSE:GNRC) to $235 from $210, maintaining a Buy rating, driven by strong growth prospects in the Commercial & Industrial (C&I) segment, particularly from data center orders. Despite recent Q4 2025 financial results missing analyst expectations (EPS of $1.61 vs. $1.77 forecast, revenue of $1.1 billion vs. $1.16 billion forecast), investor optimism for strategic updates and future growth led to a pre-market stock increase. The revised price target is based on estimated 2027 EBITDA at a 14-15x multiple, contrasting with the current InvestingPro EV/EBITDA multiple of 29.87x which suggests the stock might be overvalued.

feed.title: Justice Department Antitrust Chief Slater Resigns
feed.summary: Abigail Slater, the Justice Department's top antitrust official, resigned on Thursday, raising questions about the future of competition policy in the Trump administration. Her resignation occurs just weeks before a significant trial against Live Nation Entertainment Inc. over its Ticketmaster ticketing arm. Slater announced her departure via a statement on X.

feed.title: Deere (DE) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Deere (DE) is expected to announce its earnings for the quarter ended January 2026, with Wall Street anticipating a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues. Based on a positive Earnings ESP of +0.13% and a Zacks Rank of #3, Deere is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate of $1.92 per share. Investors will be watching the actual results closely to see how they compare to these estimates and what impact they have on the stock price.

feed.title: DA Davidson Adjusts Price Target on Akamai Technologies to $125 From $115, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: DA Davidson has increased its price target for Akamai Technologies (AKAM) to $125 from $115, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other recent analyst activities, including Oppenheimer also raising their price target and Morgan Stanley upgrading their rating on Akamai. The company's stock is showing positive performance with an over 21% change year-to-date.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Smurfit Westrock to $58 From $52, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Smurfit Westrock (NYSE: SW) to $58 from $52, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued analyst confidence in the paper packaging solutions company. Other firms like Truist Securities, Stifel, RBC, BofA, Citigroup, BNP Paribas, and Wells Fargo have also updated their price targets, generally raising them and maintaining positive ratings.

feed.title: Ancora Holdings could see multiple ways to win in Warner Bros. fight
feed.summary: Cleveland-based activist investor Ancora Holdings Group is making waves in the Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. takeover fight, advocating for a rejection of Netflix's offer in favor of Paramount Skydance Corp. The firm, known for its strategic activism, seeks "multiple ways to win" in high-profile M&A situations, distinguishing itself through both aggressive public campaigns and behind-the-scenes collaborations. Ancora's activist fund, Bellator, saw a significant gain in 2025, solidifying its reputation as a top-tier activist firm with $11 billion in assets under management.

feed.title: Renaissance Loads Up on Netflix, Lam Research, ServiceNow — Smart Money Signals Shift
feed.summary: Hedge fund Renaissance Group significantly increased its holdings in Netflix, Lam Research, and ServiceNow, signaling a strategic shift towards streaming, AI infrastructure, and enterprise software. Beyond tech, Renaissance also made new investments in healthcare, financial services, and commodities, indicating a broader diversification across sectors. These moves suggest that institutional investors are focusing on high-growth tech enablers and expanding into new opportunities.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an earnings release anticipated on February 19. The company has a positive Earnings ESP of +1.02% and a Zacks Rank of #3, indicating a high probability of beating the consensus EPS estimate. Over the last four quarters, Akamai has consistently surpassed EPS estimates.

feed.title: Here’s Why Analog Devices (ADI) Rose in Q4
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) saw its stock rise in Q4 2025, reaching new all-time highs, according to Carillon Tower Advisers' investor letter. The semiconductor company's robust earnings performance suggests that the analog cycle is past its bottom and poised for an early recovery in 2026. Despite ADI not being among the top 30 most popular stocks for hedge funds, its strong recent performance and positive outlook from investment firms highlight its potential for continued growth.

feed.title: Celebrity Cruises Earns Forbes Travel Guide’s First-Ever Five-Star Rating for a Restaurant at Sea
feed.summary: Celebrity Cruises has achieved a new milestone as Forbes Travel Guide awarded its first-ever Five-Star rating for a restaurant at sea to Chef Daniel Boulud's Le Voyage on Celebrity Xcel. This builds on Celebrity's prior recognition as the first cruise line to earn a Forbes Travel Guide Star Award in 2023. The rating underscores Celebrity Cruises' commitment to elevated hospitality and exceptional culinary experiences.

feed.title: Affiliated Managers Group, Inc. announces an Equity Buyback for 4,200,000 shares.
feed.summary: Affiliated Managers Group, Inc. (NYSE:AMG) has announced a share repurchase program to buy back up to 4,200,000 shares of its common stock. The program has no specified expiration date, indicating a flexible approach to managing its share capital. This move follows other recent corporate activities, including earnings calls, dividend announcements, and acquisitions.

feed.title: 3M class action claims company knowingly contaminated Georgia’s water supply
feed.summary: Five Georgia cities have filed a class action lawsuit against 3M Company, alleging that the company knowingly contaminated the state’s water supply with "forever chemicals" (PFAS). The lawsuit claims 3M manufactured these chemicals and was aware of their dangers and persistence in the environment for decades before agreeing to phase them out under EPA pressure. The cities are seeking declaratory and injunctive relief, as well as statutory and punitive damages for themselves and other governmental entities whose wastewater treatment facilities have been contaminated.

feed.title: Truist Securities Adjusts Smurfit Westrock PT to $60 From $49, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Truist Securities has adjusted its price target for Smurfit Westrock (SW) to $60, up from $49, while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock. This update reflects a positive outlook from the firm regarding the paper packaging solutions company. Other financial institutions like Jefferies, Stifel, RBC, BofA, Citigroup, BNP Paribas, and Wells Fargo have also recently updated their price targets and ratings for Smurfit Westrock, generally indicating a positive sentiment.

feed.title: India's Antitrust Watchdog Penalizes Intel $3.3 Million Over Discriminatory Warranty Policy
feed.summary: India's Competition Commission has fined Intel $3.3 million for a discriminatory warranty policy concerning its boxed microprocessors. The policy, effective April 2016, only honored warranties for products purchased from authorized Indian distributors, unlike its global policy. Intel has since discontinued this policy, which was a mitigating factor in the final penalty amount.

feed.title: Li Bang International Corporation Inc. Announces Topping-Out of Intelligent Kitchen Production Facility, Expects Operations to Begin in June 2026
feed.summary: Li Bang International Corporation Inc. announced the topping-out of its Phase II intelligent kitchen production facility in Gushan Town, Jiangyin, on January 28, 2026. This project, with an investment of approximately RMB 200 million, is expected to start operations in June 2026. Once fully operational, the facility will produce around 5,000 sets of intelligent kitchen equipment annually, significantly boosting the company's manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: Duke Energy's new AI hunts fake bills and scam hotlines
feed.summary: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) has implemented an AI system to identify and remove online scams targeting its customers, such as fake advertisements and imposter helplines. This initiative aims to protect customers from fraudulent schemes, especially following thousands of scam reports in 2025 and a notable spike in February. The company advises customers to be vigilant, verify payment requests, and report suspicious activities to protect themselves from urgent or threatening scam tactics.

feed.title: Cdw stock hits 52-week low at 123.05 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: CDW Corp's stock has dropped to a new 52-week low of $123.05, representing a 27.49% decrease over the past year. Despite this downturn, the company's management is actively buying back shares, and recent Q4 2025 earnings exceeded expectations, suggesting potential undervaluation according to Fair Value models and analyst targets. Investors can find more detailed insights through InvestingPro's comprehensive research report on CDW.

feed.title: State Street Corporation's State Street Investment Management Unveils New Actively Managed Prime Money Market Etf
feed.summary: State Street Investment Management, a division of State Street Corporation, has launched the State Street Prime Money Market ETF (MMK). This actively managed ETF aims to provide investors with a low-cost option for cash management, focusing on maximizing current income while preserving capital and liquidity. The ETF, priced at 18 bps, invests in a diverse portfolio of short-term, high-quality debt obligations, leveraging State Street's extensive experience in cash management.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Sees Significant Surge in Call Options Act
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) has experienced a significant surge in bullish call option activity, trading at five times the expected volume, indicating heightened investor interest and potential price movement. The company, a dominant operator of integrated resorts in Asia, shows robust financial health with strong revenue growth and profitability. Despite a high debt-to-equity ratio, valuation metrics suggest a fair stock price and analysts maintain a positive outlook for LVS.

feed.title: Federal Realty earnings on deck: Can REIT sustain dividend streak? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts keenly watching for sequential improvement and the sustainability of its 58-year dividend streak. Despite strong retail REIT fundamentals and positive analyst ratings, the company faces scrutiny over projected negative EPS growth for the full year. Investors will focus on leasing activity, occupancy trends, capital redeployment, and forward guidance to assess the REIT's ability to capitalize on sector tailwinds.

feed.title: FedEx Sets 2029 Financial Targets: $98B Revenue, $8B Operating Income
feed.summary: FedEx has set ambitious financial targets for its 2029 fiscal year, aiming for $98 billion in annual revenue and $8 billion in adjusted operating income. The company also targets an 8% operating margin, 11% return on invested capital, and $6 billion in adjusted free cash flow, with capital expenditures remaining low. This strategy emphasizes premium growth in high-margin sectors and efficiency improvements across its network.

feed.title: JPMorgan vs. Truist Financial: Which Bank Stock Will Win in 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares JPMorgan (JPM) and Truist Financial (TFC) to determine which is better positioned for 2026, considering interest rate dynamics, loan growth, credit quality, and fee-income trends. While both banks show promising prospects, JPMorgan's diversified franchise, expansion strategies, and higher profitability (ROE) give it an edge despite its higher valuation and lower dividend yield compared to Truist. The analysis concludes that JPMorgan appears to have more upside heading into 2026.

feed.title: HubSpot, Crocs, Zebra Technologies, Trinity Industries And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Thursday
feed.summary: Several prominent stocks, including HubSpot, Crocs, Zebra Technologies, and Trinity Industries, experienced significant gains on Thursday following strong quarterly financial results and positive guidance. HubSpot, for example, saw its shares jump 9.4% after beating analyst estimates for both earnings and revenue. Other companies like Fastly, Cognex, and Novocure also reported impressive financial performance or received FDA approvals, contributing to their stock surges.

feed.title: SLB wins integrated drilling contracts for Indonesia’s Tangkulo deepwater development
feed.summary: SLB has secured multiple offshore drilling and well services contracts from Mubadala Energy for the Tangkulo deepwater gas development in Indonesia's Andaman Sea. These contracts involve providing integrated drilling services throughout the well lifecycle to support the project, with Mubadala aiming for first gas by the end of 2028. The initiative underscores the strategic importance of the development for Indonesia's energy supply and will utilize advanced deepwater technologies.

feed.title: Down 18.1% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why KORU Medical Systems, Inc. (KRMD) Looks Ripe for a Turnaround
feed.summary: KORU Medical Systems, Inc. (KRMD) stock has declined 18.1% over the past four weeks but appears poised for a turnaround. Its Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 28.55 indicates it is in oversold territory, suggesting a potential trend reversal. Additionally, Wall Street analysts have significantly raised earnings estimates for KRMD, and the stock holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), further supporting a near-term recovery.

feed.title: Score as High as $250 Cash-Back With Amex Blue Cash Preferred’s Welcome Offer
feed.summary: American Express is offering targeted welcome bonuses for the Blue Cash Preferred Card, with some applicants potentially receiving up to $250 cash-back after meeting spending requirements. The article details how to check for these personalized offers without affecting one's credit score and outlines the card's benefits, including high cash-back rates on groceries, streaming, and gas. It also compares the card to others, highlighting its value for everyday household spending.

feed.title: GOOG stock rebounds as Alphabet clears $32 billion Wiz hurdle and sells rare 100-year bond
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares rebounded after EU regulators approved its $32 billion acquisition of cybersecurity firm Wiz, lifting a significant antitrust hurdle. The company also raised £1 billion through a rare 100-year sterling bond within a larger $31.51 billion global bond raise, signaling the increasing capital demands of the AI race. Despite this, the European Publishers Council filed an antitrust complaint regarding Google's AI Overviews, accusing it of using content without consent or fair compensation.

feed.title: Coca-Cola HBC Discloses Share Sale by New Business Director
feed.summary: Coca-Cola HBC's New Business Director, Jaak Mikkel, sold 30,000 vested ordinary shares for approximately GBP 1.37 million on February 11, 2026. This transaction, stemming from a performance share award, was disclosed in compliance with UK and EU market abuse regulations to ensure transparency. Despite the insider sale, analysts and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, maintain a positive outlook on GB:CCH stock, citing strong financial performance and strategic market positioning.

feed.title: Copart stock hits 52-week low at $37.40 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Copart Inc.'s stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $37.40, marking a 37.31% decrease over the past year. This decline follows a mixed first-quarter earnings report for fiscal year 2026, where the company exceeded EPS expectations but missed revenue forecasts. Despite these challenges, Copart has secured a new $1.25 billion credit agreement and shareholders approved all proposals at the 2025 annual meeting.

feed.title: Time to Buy the Dip on Joby Aviation Stock?
feed.summary: Joby Aviation is developing electric air taxis and is spending billions on certification and manufacturing, with a current market value of $10 billion but generating minimal revenue. Despite a recent fall in stock price, the article suggests it's not a good time to buy the dip due to heavy cash burn, potential shareholder dilution, and limited revenue potential from its projected manufacturing output. The company recently raised over $1 billion in convertible bonds and common stock to fund its operations.

feed.title: Boeing-Lockheed Rocket Experiences Issue, Spacecraft Performs on 'Nominal Trajectory'
feed.summary: A Boeing-Lockheed rocket encountered an issue, but the spacecraft is reported to be performing on a "nominal trajectory." Additionally, Unity Software reported its fourth-quarter financial results, with revenue increasing 35% year-over-year to $609 million, though the company recorded a loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Barclays initiates Microchip Technology stock coverage with Equalweight rating
feed.summary: Barclays has initiated coverage on Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) with an Equalweight rating and an $80 price target, noting the stock is currently trading above this target. The firm highlighted Microchip's moderate correlation with PMIs and its diversified sales across Data Center and Aerospace & Defense, while also expressing concerns about potential share loss in microcontrollers and elevated inventory levels. Despite these concerns, analysts predict profitability this year, and the company's liquid assets exceed short-term obligations, suggesting financial stability.

feed.title: Alliant Energy stock hits all-time high at 69.78 USD
feed.summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) stock has reached an all-time high of $69.78, reflecting a 16.16% increase over the past year. The company boasts a 3.14% dividend yield, has paid dividends for 56 consecutive years, and recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.535 per share. Analysts at BMO Capital, Wells Fargo, and BofA Securities have upgraded or initiated positive coverage, and Manu Asthana has been appointed to its Board of Directors.

feed.title: Analysts Stay Constructive on Salesforce (CRM) Despite Sector Weakness
feed.summary: Despite a cautious outlook on the software sector by analysts like Piper Sandler due to concerns about AI's impact and a significant drop in its stock price, Salesforce (CRM) continues to receive optimistic ratings. More than two-thirds of analysts recommend "Buy" or equivalent, with a consensus 1-year median price target suggesting a 71% upside. Salesforce, a major player in agentic AI and CRM, is recognized for its Customer 360 platform, although some analysts suggest other AI stocks might offer greater upside with less risk.

feed.title: B. Riley Adjusts Price Target on MSA Safety to $210 From $195, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: B. Riley has increased its price target for MSA Safety (MSA) to $210 from $195, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after the company reported its Q4 2025 earnings call. The article also lists recent news and financial updates for MSA Safety.

feed.title: White House adviser says JPMorgan's Dimon should cut credit card rates - report (JPM:NYSE)
feed.summary: White House trade adviser Peter Navarro has called credit card interest rates charged by JPMorgan Chase "criminal," urging CEO Jamie Dimon to lower them. Navarro specifically mentioned rates ranging from 22% to 30%, directing his comments at Dimon in a recent media report.

feed.title: AEP Q4: Bets Big on Transmission Growth, Utility Sees Massive Data Center Demand
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) exceeded Q4 earnings estimates with adjusted earnings of $1.19 per share and revenue of $5.314 billion, despite a slight decrease from the previous year. The utility is heavily investing in transmission and distribution infrastructure to meet a massive surge in demand from data centers and other large-load customers, particularly in Texas, and reaffirmed its strong operating earnings guidance for 2026.

feed.title: US Open: Cisco Systems slides 10% after earnings 📉 Mixed sentiments on Wall Street
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO.US) shares dropped 10% following its Q2 FY2026 earnings report, despite strong growth in revenue and EPS and significant AI infrastructure orders. Investors were reportedly disappointed by the company's forward guidance and broader macroeconomic concerns, leading to mixed sentiments on Wall Street. The report highlighted a surge in networking orders and AI infrastructure demand, but concerns remain about the durability of hyperscaler demand and potential trade policy impacts.

feed.title: Church & Dwight to Present at 2026 CAGNY Conference
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) announced it will present at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 8:00 AM EST. The presentation will be broadcast live on the company's website, with a replay available afterward. Church & Dwight, founded in 1846, is a leading U.S. producer of sodium bicarbonate and manufactures a wide range of personal care, household, and specialty products under well-known brand names.

feed.title: Arm & Hammer and Trojan maker to present at 2026 CAGNY event
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD), known for brands like ARM & HAMMER and TROJAN, announced it will present at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 8:00 AM EST. The presentation will be broadcast live on the company's website, with a replay available afterward. The company, founded in 1846, is a leading U.S. producer of sodium bicarbonate and a wide range of personal care and household products.

feed.title: Church & Dwight to Present at 2026 CAGNY Conference
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) announced it will present at the 2026 Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 8:00 AM EST. The presentation will be broadcast on the company's website, with a replay available afterward. The company, known for brands like ARM & HAMMER and OXICLEAN, also provided corporate details including 2024 revenues and net income.

feed.title: Can Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Gain Ground in the AI Chip Race?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is facing increased scrutiny in the AI chip race, with its Q4 2025 results and Q1 2026 guidance falling short of elevated analyst expectations despite strong growth. While investors were initially disappointed by the stock's 17% decline post-earnings, the company expects significant growth in the latter half of 2026 with the release of new powerful AI accelerators like the MI450. Analysts remain largely bullish on AMD, with a consensus price target suggesting a 41% upside.

feed.title: Street Reassesses Microsoft (MSFT) Amid Azure Growth and Capex Concerns
feed.summary: Stifel downgraded Microsoft's rating from Buy to Hold, citing concerns about Azure growth and aggressive AI spending leading to potential operating-margin headwinds. Despite this, most analysts have reiterated their ratings, with some seeing the recent stock correction as a buying opportunity. The market is reassessing MSFT due to slowed cloud sales growth and elevated capital expenditures, leading to a recent 14% stock correction.
